title: How to win public support for a global carbon tax
authors: Stefano Carattini; Steffen Kallbekken; Anton Orlov

tax_mentions.context: Survey experiment on support for hypothetical global carbon taxes with revenue options (per-capita dividends, income tax cuts, or funding mitigation projects).
tax_mentions.notes: Support highest for mitigation projects worldwide (65%) and varies for per-capita sharing.

tax_mentions.context: Background on France: Macron’s fuel/petrol tax is framed as unfair due to sharp price rises and lack of revenue rebates.
tax_mentions.notes: Used to motivate opposition; not tested as a survey treatment.

excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: Describes cap-and-trade permit systems rather than a tax on carbon or fuels.

inclusion_reason: Survey experiment identifies determinants of public support for hypothetical global carbon taxes, comparing revenue recycling options (dividends, income tax cuts, mitigation spending) across countries, directly measuring support.
